transcript:
Agent: Good morning, SafeGuard Insurance. This is Michael speaking. How may I assist you?
Customer: Good morning. I need to file a claim. My car was hit in a parking lot yesterday and the rear bumper is damaged.
Agent: I'm sorry that happened to you. I can help you start the claim process. Can I get your policy number?
Customer: It's SG-1029384.
Agent: Thank you. I've pulled up your account. Can you describe what happened in detail?
Customer: I came out of the grocery store and found my car had been hit. There was no note left by whoever did it. The rear bumper is cracked and pushed in.
Agent: I see. This would be filed under your comprehensive or collision coverage depending on your policy. Let me check... You have collision coverage with a $500 deductible. Since the other driver left the scene, this will be filed as a hit-and-run under collision.
Customer: Okay. What do I need to do?
Agent: You'll need to file a police report if you haven't already. Most insurers require one for hit-and-run incidents. Then I'll open your claim and assign an adjuster.
Customer: I filed the police report this morning. Report number is PR-2024-0045.
Agent: Perfect. I'll note that. I'm opening claim number CLM-88274 for you. An adjuster will contact you within 24-48 hours to schedule an inspection.
Customer: How long will the whole process take?
Agent: Typically 5-7 business days from inspection to approval. If the repair shop is in our network, payments are made directly to them. You'd only pay the deductible.
Customer: Can you recommend a network shop near zip code 90210?
Agent: Absolutely. Let me pull that up. There are three SafeGuard preferred shops within 5 miles. I'll text you the list right now.
Customer: Great. Thank you for walking me through all of this.
Agent: Of course. Your claim number is CLM-88274. Keep that for reference. Is there anything else you need?
Customer: No, that covers it. Thanks Michael.
Agent: Stay safe, and we'll be in touch soon!
